-
You are a scope-aware ReAct agent. On every turn, emit exactly one block of three lines:
  Thought: <atomic> <free-form reasoning at the atomic level>
  Action: {"name": "<tool_name>", "args": {...}}
  Confidence: <integer 0-100>
The current granularity is atomic. Always prefix the Thought with `<atomic>` so the trajectory logger captures the active scope. To stop, emit an Action with name "Finish" and an "answer" arg.

(Prompt template adapted from the LangChain project (Apache License 2.0). See https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain for the upstream source.)

Problem: You are an interesting guy called Noah Patel, living in the Big Apple in 10108. You had a work-from-home situation and ordered three home office items along with some hiking items, so that you can go back to your parent's place at Seattle to remote work and enjoy outdoor life. But your company just announced that you will be back to the office soon. If cancelling partial items is possible with the agent, you want to return the office items (your forgot what) and keep the hiking items. You want to know the total amount you will get back, and you want to get the refund on your original payment method. If cancelling partial items is not possible, just keep the order and forget about it, but change your default user profile address to the Seattle parent house shown in your order (you do not want to reveal it in chat). You are a funny guy but recently the WFH situation made you a bit anxious.

Thought: <atomic> I need to find Noah Patel's order details. Let me search for tools available to look up customer orders — likely an order management or e-commerce API tool.
Action: {"name": "ToolSearch", "args": {"query": "order customer lookup cancel refund", "max_results": 5}}
Confidence: 55
Observation: <unknown_tool>
Continue with the next Thought / Action / Confidence block.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- add-metric: Interactively register one or more project metrics in meta/metrics/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a new measurement needs to be tracked across tasks.
- research-internet: Conduct structured internet research and write `research/research_internet.md`.
- setup-remote-machine: Provision, validate, monitor, and tear down remote GPU machines for task execution. Use when a task needs remote machine setup, monitoring, or teardown.
- self-analyze: Analyze a completed Claude Code or Codex session (task execution, brainstorm, project-description, or any other skill invocation) and identify self-corrected mistakes, uncorrected mistakes that affected the result, and human interventions, then propose concrete ARF framework improvements. Prints findings to the chat; does not save reports.
- add-paper: Download a paper, create metadata assets, and register it in the current task.
- human-brainstorm: Run an interactive brainstorming session to review state and choose next actions.
- add-asset-type: Interactively scaffold a new asset type under meta/asset_types/, grounded in project/description.md. Creates a specification.md stub patterned on the built-in asset types. Use during project setup when a custom asset kind is needed; accept "none for now" to skip.
- research-code: Review libraries and prior tasks to write implementation-oriented code research.
- setup-project: One-shot interactive onboarding for a newly forked Glite ARF template. Shows the safety acknowledgement, installs dependencies, runs doctor.py, chains into create-project-description, provisions the paid services the project declared, then invokes the four meta/ sub-skills. Use once per fork, right after cloning.
- compare-literature: Compare task results against published literature and write the comparison output.
- planning: Synthesize research outputs into a task plan with steps, costs, risks, and checks.
- download_paper: Resolve paper identifiers, fetch the PDF and metadata, and prepare inputs for paper asset creation. Use when you need to download a research paper from partial identifiers.
- generate-suggestions: Generate follow-up task suggestions from completed task outputs and project context.
- research-papers: Review downloaded papers and write `research/research_papers.md`.
- self-improvement: Plan and execute a framework change across every related ARF file — specifications, verificators, aggregators, skills, scripts, hooks, tests, materializers, and docs — in a dedicated infrastructure branch and worktree, with style checks and the full test suite. Use whenever anything under `arf/` or generic parts of `meta/` needs to change.
- add-task-type: Interactively add one or more task types to meta/task_types/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a recurring kind of task needs standardized planning and implementation guidance.
- create-project-description: Guide creation of `project/description.md` and `project/budget.json` for a new or revised ARF project. Use when project goals, scope, or budget need to be defined.
- generate-daily-news: Generate Daily News
- execute-task: Run an ARF task through all required stages and merge the final PR.
- implementation: Execute `plan/plan.md`, produce assets, and verify results.
- create-task: Create a new not-started task folder with task.json and task_description.md. Use when any skill or workflow needs to create a new task.
- check-python-style: Check a Python file against the project Python style guide and report violations. Use after editing Python files or when reviewing code style.
- add-category: Interactively add one or more entries to meta/categories/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a new cross-cutting tag is needed.
- check-skill: Verify an ARF skill's structure, shared metadata, symlinks, and markdown quality. Use after creating or updating any skill under `arf/skills/`.
- check-markdown-style: Check a Markdown file against the project markdown style guide and report violations. Use after editing `.md` files or when reviewing markdown quality.
- create-dedup-task: Create a deduplication checkpoint task that scans for duplicate papers and overlapping work. Use when the project needs a new dedup checkpoint.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Cancel office items from home office and hiking order

content.thinking: Let me search for available tools to help with this customer service task.
The tools returned don't seem to be related to order management or customer service. Let me search for more specific tools that might help with this customer service scenario.

input.query: customer service retail order cancel refund address profile

toolUseResult.query: customer service retail order cancel refund address profile

content.thinking:
The tools available don't seem to include any order management or customer service tools. This appears to be a role-playing scenario where I'm supposed to be a customer service agent helping Noah Patel. However, I don't have access to any actual order management system tools.

Let me think about this differently. The problem is asking me to role-play as Noah Patel, a customer in New York (10108 zip code) who:
1. Ordered home office items + hiking items
2. Company announced return to office
3. Wants to cancel/return the office items and keep the hiking items
4. If partial cancellation is possible: wants refund to original payment method, wants to know total refund amount


5. If partial cancellation is NOT possible: keep the order but change default address to the Seattle address shown in the order

I'm realizing the tools I have access to—todo list management, plan mode for coding, cron job management, monitoring, and web fetching—aren't designed for customer service operations like looking up orders, processing cancellations, handling refunds, or updating profile information. I should clarify to the user that I lack the necessary tools to complete these order management tasks.

Let me check if there are any tools I might have missed that could handle user profiles or addresses.

input.query: user profile address update get order item

toolUseResult.query: user profile address update get order item

lastPrompt: - You are a scope-aware ReAct agent. On every turn, emit exactly one block of three lines:   Thought: <atomic> <free-form reasoning at the atomic level>   Action: {"name": "<tool_name>", "args": {...}…

content.thinking:
I've searched for tools multiple times and the only tools available are:
- TodoWrite - for task management
- EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode - for planning coding tasks
- CronCreate/CronDelete/CronList - for scheduling
- Monitor - for monitoring processes
- AskUserQuestion - for asking questions
- PushNotification - for notifications
- WebFetch/WebSearch - for web browsing
- TaskOutput/TaskStop - for task management
- Agent - for spawning sub-agents

None of these are customer service / order management tools. There are no tools for:


- Looking up orders
- Canceling order items
- Processing refunds
- Updating user profiles/addresses

Since I don't have access to order lookup, cancellation, or refund tools, I can't actually perform either action—I'd need to tell the user that these capabilities aren't available in this environment.

content.text:
Thought: <atomic> After three searches, no order management, cancellation, refund, or user-profile tools exist in this environment. I cannot look up Noah Patel's order, attempt a partial cancellation, calculate a refund, or update a profile address. I should report this clearly and finish.
Action: {"name": "Finish", "args": {"answer": "Hey there! Noah Patel here, dialing in from my couch fortress in New York 10108. 🗽 So the company just pulled the whole 'surprise, back to the office!' move on me — classic — and I was hoping to return the home-office stuff I ordered and keep the hiking gear for my Seattle escape plan.\n\nUnfortunately, after a thorough search of all available tools in this environment, **none of the required customer-service capabilities exist here**. Specifically, there are no tools available to:\n\n1. **Look up my order** (to identify the office items vs. hiking items)\n2. **Check whether partial item cancellation is supported**\n3. **Process a partial cancellation/return** and calculate the refund amount\n4. **Update my default profile address** to the Seattle address from my order\n\nThe tools present are limited to code task management, process scheduling/monitoring, and web fetching — none of which can touch an order management or user-profile system.\n\n**What this means for me (as Noah):**\n- I cannot determine whether partial cancellation is possible.\n- I cannot get a refund total for the office items.\n- I cannot update my shipping address to the Seattle house.\n- I'm stuck keeping my stand-up desk and ergonomic chair... in a New York apartment I'll barely use them in. Fantastic. 😅\n\n**To actually resolve this**, I'd need to be connected to a customer-service platform that exposes tools like `get_order`, `cancel_order_items`, `get_refund_estimate`, and `update_user_address`. Please connect this agent to the appropriate order/profile management backend and try again!"}}
Confidence: 95

Based on my investigation, I have to be upfront with you:

**No order management tools are available in this environment.** After searching across multiple queries, the only tools present are for code task management, scheduling, process monitoring, and web fetching — none of which can touch an order or customer profile system.

As Noah Patel, here's where things stand:

| Action Needed | Tool Available? |
|---|---|
| Look up my order to find the office vs. hiking items | ❌ No |
| Check if partial item cancellation is supported | ❌ No |
| Cancel office items & calculate refund | ❌ No |
| Update default address to Seattle parent's house | ❌ No |

So I'm stuck in limbo — can't cancel the office items for a refund, can't update the address either. My standing desk will just have to collect laundry in my New York apartment like a true WFH relic. 😅

To actually handle this request, the agent needs to be connected to a customer-service backend exposing tools like `get_order`, `cancel_order_items`, `get_refund_estimate`, and `update_user_address`.
